title: Afeyan, Nagler & Tu (2025): Spatial RCC analysis: Clone Enrichment In Spatial Compartments
text:
Introduction
This is a book that contains all code to reproduce figures related to spatial analysis of TCR data associated with Afeyan, Nagler & Tu, et al (2025)
Note that while the code is identical, this book was rendered after final manuscript figures were generated and thus minor differences may arise, particularly for steps that are non-deterministic or rely on random number generators.

title: Afeyan, Nagler & Tu (2025): Spatial RCC analysis: Clone Enrichment In Spatial Compartments
section: Tertiary lymphoid structures are reservoirs of stem-like tumor-specific T cells in renal cell carcinoma
text:
Tertiary lymphoid structures are reservoirs of stem-like tumor-specific T cells in renal cell carcinoma
Alexander B. Afeyan†, Adi Nagler†, Chloe R. Tu†, Amit Sud, Nourhan El Ahmar, Gabriel Roberti De Oliveira, Berkay Simsek, Mehdi Borji, Haley E. Sax, Cleo Forman, Sophia Liu, Patrick A. Ott, Toni K. Choueiri, Jennifer G. Abelin, Shuqiang Li, Kenneth J. Livak, Jeremy M. Simon, Derin B. Keskin, Fei Chen, Sabina Signoretti, Giacomo Oliveira‡ˆ, David A. Braun‡ˆ, Catherine J. Wu‡ˆ

Abstract
Tertiary lymphoid structures (TLS) are associated with improved response to immune checkpoint blockade across solid tumors1–3, but their impact on tumor-specific T cell responses remains incompletely defined. By tracking the spatial phenotype of T cell clones with confirmed anti-tumor specificity, we report the architecture of T cell responses in renal cell carcinoma (RCC). Across 24 RCC tumors, TLS-containing (TLS+) tumors were more infiltrated by exhausted CD8 T cells, but with a reduced terminal exhaustion transcriptional program compared to TLS- tumors. To investigate the effect of TLS localization on RCC-specific T cells, we screened 581 CD8+ and CD4+ T cell receptor (TCR) α/β pairs across six patients and identified 72 tumor-specific (TS) and 21 tumor-antigen-specific TCRs. Among these bona fide TS-TCR clonotypes, 45.8% of clonotypes infiltrated tumors, and 10.2% localized within TLS by spatial transcriptomics. TS-TCRs in TLS+ tumors preferentially colocalized with plasma cell transcripts (P<0.0001) compared to viral-reactive TCRs, and were associated with upregulated progenitor exhausted. However, in counterbalance, exhausted TCR clonotypes also colocalized with macrophages of inferred immunosuppressive phenotype concentrated within a myeloid boundary of the tumor (P=3x10-14). Our data reveal TLS as reservoirs of tumor-specific T cells with stem-like features that could be leveraged by T cell immunotherapies.

title: 1  102T Boundary: Clone enrichment
text:
2.4 Permutation test logic:
Hypothesis: Our null hypothesis claims that TCR_X is not more enriched in the boundary than any other TCR The alternative hypothesis claims that TCR_Xs is more enriched in the boundary than any other TCR
Take into account the non-independent variable of puck identity by permuting sample label within the puck. e.g. say Puck30 has 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 has 20 TCR_X beads. After permutation, Puck30 will still have 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 20 TCR_X beads.
Test statistic:
Enrichment = (number of beads for TCR_X in the structure/total number of beads for TCR_X across all structures)/(number of beads for all other clonotypes in the structure/ total number of beads for all other clonotypes across all structures) - 1
In other words, enrichment = (fraction of TCR_X in the boundary) / (fraction of all other clones in the boundary) - 1

2.5 Get TCRs seen at a count of 5 or more across all pucks

# Remove metadata columns that other pucks don't have
P102TA_17_RNA@meta.data <- P102TA_17_RNA@meta.data %>%
  select(-largest_clone_id, -puck17_count, -log_puck17_count)
P102TA_10_RNA@meta.data <- P102TA_10_RNA@meta.data %>%
  select(-largest_clone_id, -puck10_count, -log_puck10_count)

P102TA_md <- do.call(rbind, list(P102TA_10_RNA@meta.data, P102TA_17_RNA@meta.data, P102TA_33_RNA@meta.data))

unique_clones <- get_unique_productive_clones(P102TA_md$clone_id)

P102TA_filtered_clones <- count_clones_by_annotated_area(P102TA_md, unique_clones) %>%
  group_by(clone_id) %>%
  summarize(sum_count = sum(count)) %>%
  filter(sum_count >= 5) %>%
  pull(clone_id)

length(P102TA_filtered_clones)

P102TA_md <- P102TA_md %>%
  filter(!is.na(clone_id))

title: 1  102T Boundary: Clone enrichment
text:
2.7 Permute

# Save results in a list
boundary_clone_results.list <- list()

for(clone in P102TA_filtered_clones){
  P102TA_TCRX_md <- P102TA_md %>%
    # Select columns of interest
    select(orig.ident, clone_id, barcode, area) %>%
    # Label TCR_X containing beads as "interesting" and label all other beads as "other".
    mutate(tcr_label = case_when(str_detect(clone_id, paste0(clone, "($|,)")) ~ "TCR_of_interest",
                                 T ~ "other_tcr"),
           # Label the boundary as "interesting" and other areas as "other"
           area_label = case_when(area == given_area ~ "area_of_interest",
                                  T ~ "other_area"))
  
  # Find pucks in which TCR_X is not observed
  P102TA_TCRX_pucks <- P102TA_TCRX_md %>%
    filter(tcr_label == "TCR_of_interest") %>%
    distinct(orig.ident) %>%
    pull(orig.ident)
  
  # Remove pucks where TCR_X is not observed
  P102TA_TCRX_md <- P102TA_TCRX_md %>%
    filter(orig.ident %in% P102TA_TCRX_pucks)
  
  # Test enrichment of TCR_X in the boundary
  boundary_results <- enrichment_permutation_test(P102TA_TCRX_md, n_perm = n_perm, clonotype = clone)
  boundary_results$pval
  
  # Print plot only if the clone is significant
  if(boundary_results$pval <= 0.05){
    # Plot the fraction of TCR_X in TLS vs all other TCRs in TLS for each puck.
    # In other words, plot Cc/Call and Ac/Aall
    plot <- P102TA_TCRX_md %>%
      group_by(tcr_label, area_label, orig.ident) %>%
      dplyr::count() %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = area_label, values_from = "n") %>%
      # Some values are supposed to be 0 but are NA due to dplyr::count
      replace(is.na(.), 0) %>%
      # Calculate Call and Aall
      mutate(all_areas = sum(area_of_interest, other_area),
      # Calculate Cc/Call and Ac/All
             fraction = area_of_interest/all_areas) %>%
      select(tcr_label, fraction, orig.ident) %>%
      ggplot() +
      geom_boxplot(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, fill = tcr_label)) +
      geom_point(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, color = orig.ident)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("TCR of interest: ", clone)) +
      ylab("Fraction in the boundary")
    
    print(plot)
  }
  
  # KEEP THIS ORDER, or the names of the list will be in the wrong order later
  boundary_clone_results.list <- append(boundary_clone_results.list, list(boundary_results))
}

In other words, enrichment = (fraction of TCR_X in the boundary) / (fraction of all other clones in the boundary) - 1
Note: this logic applies to specific clonotypes, phenotypes of clones, and specificity of clones

# Remove metadata columns that other pucks don't have
P102TA_17_RNA@meta.data <- P102TA_17_RNA@meta.data %>%
  select(-largest_clone_id, -puck17_count, -log_puck17_count)
P102TA_10_RNA@meta.data <- P102TA_10_RNA@meta.data %>%
  select(-largest_clone_id, -puck10_count, -log_puck10_count)

P102TA_md <- do.call(rbind, list(P102TA_10_RNA@meta.data, P102TA_17_RNA@meta.data, P102TA_33_RNA@meta.data))

# These two functions work for grabbing phenotypes too- with some changes.
unique_phenotypes <- get_unique_productive_clones(P102TA_md$PrimaryCluster)

# Rename PrimaryCluster to clone_id so count_clones_by_annotated_area() works
temp <- P102TA_md %>%
  select(PrimaryCluster, area) %>%
  rename("clone_id" = "PrimaryCluster")

P102TA_filtered_phenotypes <- count_clones_by_annotated_area(temp, unique_phenotypes) %>%
  group_by(clone_id) %>%
  summarize(sum_count = sum(count)) %>%
  filter(sum_count >= 5) %>%
  pull(clone_id)

title: 2  102T Boundary: Phenotype enrichment
text:
3.7 Permute

# Save results in a list
boundary_phenotype_results.list <- list()

for(phenotype in P102TA_filtered_phenotypes){
  P102TA_phenotypeX_md <- P102TA_md %>%
    # Select columns of interest
    select(orig.ident, PrimaryCluster, barcode, area) %>%
    # Label phenotype_X containing beads as "interesting" and label all other beads as "other".
    # note: spatial-unique clonotypes will be labelled as "other". str_detect IS case sensititve
    mutate(tcr_label = case_when(str_detect(PrimaryCluster, paste0(phenotype, "($|,)")) ~ "TCR_of_interest",
                                 T ~ "other_tcr"),
           # Label the boundary as "interesting" and other areas as "other"
           area_label = case_when(area == given_area ~ "area_of_interest",
                                  T ~ "other_area"))
  
  # Find pucks in which phenotype_X is observed
  P102TA_phenotypeX_pucks <- P102TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(tcr_label == "TCR_of_interest") %>%
    distinct(orig.ident) %>%
    pull(orig.ident)
  
  # Filter for pucks where phenotype_X is observed
  P102TA_phenotypeX_md <- P102TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(orig.ident %in% P102TA_phenotypeX_pucks)
  
  # Test enrichment of phenotype_X in the boundary
  boundary_results <- enrichment_permutation_test(P102TA_phenotypeX_md, n_perm = n_perm, clonotype = phenotype)
  boundary_results$pval
  
  # Print plot only if the phenotype is significant
  if(boundary_results$pval <= 0.05){
    # Plot the fraction of beads with phenotype_X in TLS vs all other phenotypes in TLS for each puck.
    # In other words, plot Cc/Call and Ac/Aall
    plot <- P102TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
      group_by(tcr_label, area_label, orig.ident) %>%
      dplyr::count() %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = area_label, values_from = "n") %>%
      # Some values are supposed to be 0 but are NA due to dplyr::count
      replace(is.na(.), 0) %>%
      # Calculate Call and Aall
      mutate(all_areas = sum(area_of_interest, other_area),
      # Calculate Cc/Call and Ac/All
             fraction = area_of_interest/all_areas) %>%
      select(tcr_label, fraction, orig.ident) %>%
      ggplot() +
      geom_boxplot(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, fill = tcr_label)) +
      geom_point(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, color = orig.ident)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("Phenotype of interest: ", phenotype)) +
      ylab("Fraction in the boundary")
    
    print(plot)
  }
  
  # KEEP THIS ORDER, or the names of the list will be in the wrong order later
  boundary_phenotype_results.list <- append(boundary_phenotype_results.list, list(boundary_results))
}

title: 3  102T TLS: Clone enrichment
text:
4.4 Permutation test logic:
Hypothesis: Our null hypothesis claims that TCR_X is not more enriched in the TLS than any other TCR The alternative hypothesis claims that TCR_Xs is more enriched in the TLS than any other TCR
Take into account the non-independent variable of puck identity by permuting sample label within the puck. e.g. say Puck30 has 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 has 20 TCR_X beads. After permutation, Puck30 will still have 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 20 TCR_X beads.
Test statistic:
Enrichment = (number of beads for TCR_X in the structure/total number of beads for TCR_X across all structures)/(number of beads for all other clonotypes in the structure/ total number of beads for all other clonotypes across all structures) - 1
In other words, enrichment = (fraction of TCR_X in the TLS) / (fraction of all other clones in the TLS) - 1

title: 3  102T TLS: Clone enrichment
text:
4.7 Run the permutation test

# Save results in a list
tls_clone_results.list <- list()

for(clone in P102TA_filtered_clones){
  P102TA_TCRX_md <- P102TA_md %>%
    # Select columns of interest
    select(orig.ident, clone_id, barcode, area) %>%
    # Label TCR_X containing beads as "interesting" and label all other beads as "other".
    mutate(tcr_label = case_when(str_detect(clone_id, paste0(clone, "($|,)")) ~ "TCR_of_interest",
                                 T ~ "other_tcr"),
           # Label the TLS as "interesting" and other areas as "other"
           area_label = case_when(area == given_area ~ "area_of_interest",
                                  T ~ "other_area"))
  
  # Find pucks in which TCR_X is not observed
  P102TA_TCRX_pucks <- P102TA_TCRX_md %>%
    filter(tcr_label == "TCR_of_interest") %>%
    distinct(orig.ident) %>%
    pull(orig.ident)
  
  # Remove pucks where TCR_X is not observed
  P102TA_TCRX_md <- P102TA_TCRX_md %>%
    filter(orig.ident %in% P102TA_TCRX_pucks)
  
  # Test enrichment of TCR_X in the TLS
  tls_results <- enrichment_permutation_test(P102TA_TCRX_md, n_perm = n_perm, clonotype = clone)
  tls_results$pval
  
  # Print plot only if the clone is significant
  if(tls_results$pval <= 0.05){
    # Plot the fraction of TCR_X in TLS vs all other TCRs in TLS for each puck.
    # In other words, plot Cc/Call and Ac/Aall
    plot <- P102TA_TCRX_md %>%
      group_by(tcr_label, area_label, orig.ident) %>%
      dplyr::count() %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = area_label, values_from = "n") %>%
      # Some values are supposed to be 0 but are NA due to dplyr::count
      replace(is.na(.), 0) %>%
      # Calculate Call and Aall
      mutate(all_areas = sum(area_of_interest, other_area),
      # Calculate Cc/Call and Ac/All
             fraction = area_of_interest/all_areas) %>%
      select(tcr_label, fraction, orig.ident) %>%
      ggplot() +
      geom_boxplot(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, fill = tcr_label)) +
      geom_point(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, color = orig.ident)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("TCR of interest: ", clone)) +
      ylab("Fraction in the TLS")
    
    print(plot)
  }
  
  # KEEP THIS ORDER, or the names of the list will be in the wrong order later
  tls_clone_results.list <- append(tls_clone_results.list, list(tls_results))
}

title: 4  102T Boundary: Phenotype enrichment
text:
5.4 Permutation test logic:
Hypothesis: Our null hypothesis claims that TCR_X is the same level of enrichment in the TLS compared to other TCRs The alternative hypothesis claims that TCR_Xs is differentially enriched in the TLS compared to other TCRs
Take into account the non-independent variable of puck identity by permuting sample label within the puck. e.g. say Puck30 has 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 has 20 TCR_X beads. After permutation, Puck30 will still have 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 20 TCR_X beads.
Test statistic: Enrichment = (number of beads for TCR_X in the structure/total number of beads for TCR_X across all structures)/(number of beads for all other clonotypes in the structure/ total number of beads for all other clonotypes across all structures) - 1
In other words, enrichment = (fraction of TCR_X in the TLS) / (fraction of all other clones in the TLS) - 1
Note: this logic applies to specific clonotypes, phenotypes of clones, and specificity of clones

5.5 Get phenotypes seen at a count of 5 or more across all pucks

# Remove metadata columns that other pucks don't have
P102TA_17_RNA@meta.data <- P102TA_17_RNA@meta.data %>%
  select(-largest_clone_id, -puck17_count, -log_puck17_count)
P102TA_10_RNA@meta.data <- P102TA_10_RNA@meta.data %>%
  select(-largest_clone_id, -puck10_count, -log_puck10_count)

P102TA_md <- do.call(rbind, list(P102TA_10_RNA@meta.data, P102TA_17_RNA@meta.data, P102TA_33_RNA@meta.data))

# These two functions work for grabbing phenotypes too- with some changes.
unique_phenotypes <- get_unique_productive_clones(P102TA_md$PrimaryCluster)

# Rename PrimaryCluster to clone_id so count_clones_by_annotated_area() works
temp <- P102TA_md %>%
  select(PrimaryCluster, area) %>%
  rename("clone_id" = "PrimaryCluster")

P102TA_filtered_phenotypes <- count_clones_by_annotated_area(temp, unique_phenotypes) %>%
  group_by(clone_id) %>%
  summarize(sum_count = sum(count)) %>%
  filter(sum_count >= 5) %>%
  pull(clone_id)

5.6 Rename individual TLS and filter for beads containing TCRs

# Rename individual TLS to "TLS"
P102TA_md <- P102TA_md %>%
  mutate(area = case_when(area %in% c("TLS1", "TLS2", "TLS3", "M2") ~ "TLS",

title: 4  102T Boundary: Phenotype enrichment
text:
5.7 Permute

# Save results in a list
TLS_phenotype_results.list <- list()

for(phenotype in P102TA_filtered_phenotypes){
  P102TA_phenotypeX_md <- P102TA_md %>%
    # Select columns of interest
    select(orig.ident, PrimaryCluster, barcode, area) %>%
    # Label phenotype_X containing beads as "interesting" and label all other beads as "other".
    # note: spatial-unique clonotypes will be labelled as "other" & str_detect IS case sensitive!
    mutate(tcr_label = case_when(str_detect(PrimaryCluster, paste0(phenotype, "($|,)")) ~ "TCR_of_interest",
                                 T ~ "other_tcr"),
           # Label the TLS as "interesting" and other areas as "other"
           area_label = case_when(area == given_area ~ "area_of_interest",
                                  T ~ "other_area"))
  
  # Find pucks in which phenotype_X is observed
  P102TA_phenotypeX_pucks <- P102TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(tcr_label == "TCR_of_interest") %>%
    distinct(orig.ident) %>%
    pull(orig.ident)
  
  # Filter for pucks where phenotype_X is observed
  P102TA_phenotypeX_md <- P102TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(orig.ident %in% P102TA_phenotypeX_pucks)

  # Test enrichment of phenotype_X in the TLS
  TLS_results <- enrichment_permutation_test(P102TA_phenotypeX_md, n_perm = n_perm, clonotype = phenotype)
  TLS_results$pval
  
  # Print plot only if the phenotype is significant
  if(TLS_results$pval <= 0.05){
    # Plot the fraction of beads with phenotype_X in TLS vs all other phenotypes in TLS for each puck.
    # In other words, plot Cc/Call and Ac/Aall
    plot <- P102TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
      group_by(tcr_label, area_label, orig.ident) %>%
      dplyr::count() %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = area_label, values_from = "n") %>%
      # Some values are supposed to be 0 but are NA due to dplyr::count
      replace(is.na(.), 0) %>%
      # Calculate Call and Aall
      mutate(all_areas = sum(area_of_interest, other_area),
      # Calculate Cc/Call and Ac/All
             fraction = area_of_interest/all_areas) %>%
      select(tcr_label, fraction, orig.ident) %>%
      ggplot() +
      geom_boxplot(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, fill = tcr_label)) +
      geom_point(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, color = orig.ident)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("Phenotype of interest: ", phenotype)) +
      ylab("Fraction in the TLS")
    
    print(plot)
  }
  
  # KEEP THIS ORDER, or the names of the list will be in the wrong order later
  TLS_phenotype_results.list <- append(TLS_phenotype_results.list, list(TLS_results))
}

In other words, enrichment = (fraction of TCR_X in the tumor) / (fraction of all other clones in the tumor) - 1

title: 5  102T Tumor: Clone enrichment
text:
6.7 Permute

# Save results in a list
tumor_clone_results.list <- list()

for(clone in P102TA_filtered_clones){
  P102TA_TCRX_md <- P102TA_md %>%
    # Select columns of interest
    select(orig.ident, clone_id, barcode, area) %>%
    # Label TCR_X containing beads as "interesting" and label all other beads as "other".
    mutate(tcr_label = case_when(str_detect(clone_id, paste0(clone, "($|,)")) ~ "TCR_of_interest",
                                 T ~ "other_tcr"),
           # Label the tumor as "interesting" and other areas as "other"
           area_label = case_when(area == given_area ~ "area_of_interest",
                                  T ~ "other_area"))
  
  # Find pucks in which TCR_X is not observed
  P102TA_TCRX_pucks <- P102TA_TCRX_md %>%
    filter(tcr_label == "TCR_of_interest") %>%
    distinct(orig.ident) %>%
    pull(orig.ident)
  
  # Remove pucks where TCR_X is not observed
  P102TA_TCRX_md <- P102TA_TCRX_md %>%
    filter(orig.ident %in% P102TA_TCRX_pucks)
  
  # Test enrichment of TCR_X in the tumor
  tumor_results <- enrichment_permutation_test(P102TA_TCRX_md, n_perm = n_perm, clonotype = clone)
  tumor_results$pval
  
  # Print plot only if the clone is significant
  if(tumor_results$pval <= 0.05){
    # Plot the fraction of TCR_X in TLS vs all other TCRs in TLS for each puck.
    # In other words, plot Cc/Call and Ac/Aall
    plot <- P102TA_TCRX_md %>%
      group_by(tcr_label, area_label, orig.ident) %>%
      dplyr::count() %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = area_label, values_from = "n") %>%
      # Some values are supposed to be 0 but are NA due to dplyr::count
      replace(is.na(.), 0) %>%
      # Calculate Call and Aall
      mutate(all_areas = sum(area_of_interest, other_area),
      # Calculate Cc/Call and Ac/All
             fraction = area_of_interest/all_areas) %>%
      select(tcr_label, fraction, orig.ident) %>%
      ggplot() +
      geom_boxplot(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, fill = tcr_label)) +
      geom_point(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, color = orig.ident)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("TCR of interest: ", clone)) +
      ylab("Fraction in the tumor")
    
    print(plot)
  }
  
  # KEEP THIS ORDER, or the names of the list will be in the wrong order later
  tumor_clone_results.list <- append(tumor_clone_results.list, list(tumor_results))
}

title: 6  102T Tumor: Phenotype enrichment
text:
7.4 Permutation test logic:
Hypothesis: Our null hypothesis claims that TCR_X is not more enriched in the tumor than any other TCR The alternative hypothesis claims that TCR_Xs is more enriched in the tumor than any other TCR
Take into account the non-independent variable of puck identity by permuting sample label within the puck. e.g. say Puck30 has 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 has 20 TCR_X beads. After permutation, Puck30 will still have 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 20 TCR_X beads.
Test statistic: Enrichment = (number of beads for TCR_X in the structure/total number of beads for TCR_X across all structures)/(number of beads for all other clonotypes in the structure/ total number of beads for all other clonotypes across all structures) - 1
In other words, enrichment = (fraction of TCR_X in the tumor) / (fraction of all other clones in the tumor) - 1
Note: this logic applies to specific clonotypes, phenotypes of clones, and specificity of clones

# These two functions work for grabbing phenotypes too- with some changes.

# Rename PrimaryCluster to clone_id so count_clones_by_annotated_area() works

P102TA_filtered_phenotypes <- count_clones_by_annotated_area(temp, unique_phenotypes) %>%

# Save results in a list

for(phenotype in P102TA_filtered_phenotypes){
  P102TA_phenotypeX_md <- P102TA_md %>%
    # Select columns of interest
    select(orig.ident, PrimaryCluster, barcode, area) %>%
    # Label phenotype_X containing beads as "interesting" and label all other beads as "other".
    # note: spatial-unique clonotypes will be labelled as "other".
    mutate(tcr_label = case_when(str_detect(PrimaryCluster, paste0(phenotype, "($|,)")) ~ "TCR_of_interest",
                                 T ~ "other_tcr"),
           # Label the tumor as "interesting" and other areas as "other"
           area_label = case_when(area == given_area ~ "area_of_interest",
                                  T ~ "other_area"))
  
  # Find pucks in which phenotype_X is observed
  P102TA_phenotypeX_pucks <- P102TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(tcr_label == "TCR_of_interest") %>%
    distinct(orig.ident) %>%
    pull(orig.ident)
  
  # Filter for pucks where phenotype_X is observed
  P102TA_phenotypeX_md <- P102TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(orig.ident %in% P102TA_phenotypeX_pucks)
  
  # Test enrichment of phenotype_X in the tumor
  tumor_results <- enrichment_permutation_test(P102TA_phenotypeX_md, n_perm = n_perm, clonotype = phenotype)
  tumor_results$pval
  
  # Print plot only if the phenotype is significant
  if(tumor_results$pval <= 0.05){
    # Plot the fraction of beads with phenotype_X in TLS vs all other phenotypes in TLS for each puck.
    # In other words, plot Cc/Call and Ac/Aall
    plot <- P102TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
      group_by(tcr_label, area_label, orig.ident) %>%
      dplyr::count() %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = area_label, values_from = "n") %>%
      # Some values are supposed to be 0 but are NA due to dplyr::count
      replace(is.na(.), 0) %>%
      # Calculate Call and Aall
      mutate(all_areas = sum(area_of_interest, other_area),
      # Calculate Cc/Call and Ac/All
             fraction = area_of_interest/all_areas) %>%
      select(tcr_label, fraction, orig.ident) %>%
      ggplot() +
      geom_boxplot(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, fill = tcr_label)) +
      geom_point(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, color = orig.ident)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("Phenotype of interest: ", phenotype)) +
      ylab("Fraction in the tumor")
    
    print(plot)
  }
  
  # KEEP THIS ORDER, or the names of the list will be in the wrong order later
  tumor_phenotype_results.list <- append(tumor_phenotype_results.list, list(tumor_results))
}

title: 7  107T TLS: Clone enrichment
text:
8.4 Permutation test logic:
Hypothesis: Our null hypothesis claims that TCR_X is not more enriched in the TLS than any other TCR The alternative hypothesis claims that TCR_Xs is more enriched in the TLS than any other TCR
Take into account the non-independent variable of puck identity by permuting sample label within the puck. e.g. say Puck30 has 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 has 20 TCR_X beads. After permutation, Puck30 will still have 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 20 TCR_X beads.
Test statistic:
Enrichment = (number of beads for TCR_X in the structure/total number of beads for TCR_X across all structures)/(number of beads for all other clonotypes in the structure/ total number of beads for all other clonotypes across all structures) - 1
In other words, enrichment = (fraction of TCR_X in the TLS) / (fraction of all other clones in the TLS) - 1

P107TA_md <- P107TA_md %>%
  mutate(area = case_when(area %in% c("TLS1", "TLS2", "TLS3", "TLS4") ~ "TLS",

title: 7  107T TLS: Clone enrichment
text:
8.7 Run the permutation test

# Save results in a list
tls_clone_results.list <- list()

for(clone in P107TA_filtered_clones){
  P107TA_TCRX_md <- P107TA_md %>%
    # Select columns of interest
    select(orig.ident, clone_id, barcode, area) %>%
    # Label TCR_X containing beads as "interesting" and label all other beads as "other".
    mutate(tcr_label = case_when(str_detect(clone_id, paste0(clone, "($|,)")) ~ "TCR_of_interest",
                                 T ~ "other_tcr"),
           # Label the TLS as "interesting" and other areas as "other"
           area_label = case_when(area == given_area ~ "area_of_interest",
                                  T ~ "other_area"))
  
  # Find pucks in which TCR_X is not observed
  P107TA_TCRX_pucks <- P107TA_TCRX_md %>%
    filter(tcr_label == "TCR_of_interest") %>%
    distinct(orig.ident) %>%
    pull(orig.ident)
  
  # Remove pucks where TCR_X is not observed
  P107TA_TCRX_md <- P107TA_TCRX_md %>%
    filter(orig.ident %in% P107TA_TCRX_pucks)
  
  # Test enrichment of TCR_X in the TLS
  tls_results <- enrichment_permutation_test(P107TA_TCRX_md, n_perm = n_perm, clonotype = clone)
  tls_results$pval
  
  # Print plot only if the clone is significant
  if(tls_results$pval <= 0.05){
    # Plot the fraction of TCR_X in TLS vs all other TCRs in TLS for each puck.
    # In other words, plot Cc/Call and Ac/Aall
    plot <- P107TA_TCRX_md %>%
      group_by(tcr_label, area_label, orig.ident) %>%
      dplyr::count() %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = area_label, values_from = "n") %>%
      # Some values are supposed to be 0 but are NA due to dplyr::count
      replace(is.na(.), 0) %>%
      # Calculate Call and Aall
      mutate(all_areas = sum(area_of_interest, other_area),
      # Calculate Cc/Call and Ac/All
             fraction = area_of_interest/all_areas) %>%
      select(tcr_label, fraction, orig.ident) %>%
      ggplot() +
      geom_boxplot(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, fill = tcr_label)) +
      geom_point(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, color = orig.ident)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("TCR of interest: ", clone)) +
      ylab("Fraction in the TLS")
    
    print(plot)
  }
  
  # KEEP THIS ORDER, or the names of the list will be in the wrong order later
  tls_clone_results.list <- append(tls_clone_results.list, list(tls_results))
}

`summarise()` has grouped output by 'orig.ident'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.

title: 8  107T TLS: Phenotype enrichment
text:
9.7 Permute

# Save results in a list
TLS_phenotype_results.list <- list()

for(phenotype in P107TA_filtered_phenotypes){
  P107TA_phenotypeX_md <- P107TA_md %>%
    # Select columns of interest
    select(orig.ident, PrimaryCluster, barcode, area) %>%
    # Label phenotype_X containing beads as "interesting" and label all other beads as "other".
    # note: spatial-unique clonotypes will be labelled as "other" & str_detect IS case sensitive!
    mutate(tcr_label = case_when(str_detect(PrimaryCluster, paste0(phenotype, "($|,)")) ~ "TCR_of_interest",
                                 T ~ "other_tcr"),
           # Label the TLS as "interesting" and other areas as "other"
           area_label = case_when(area == given_area ~ "area_of_interest",
                                  T ~ "other_area"))
  
  # Find pucks in which phenotype_X is observed
  P107TA_phenotypeX_pucks <- P107TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(tcr_label == "TCR_of_interest") %>%
    distinct(orig.ident) %>%
    pull(orig.ident)
  
  # Filter for pucks where phenotype_X is observed
  P107TA_phenotypeX_md <- P107TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(orig.ident %in% P107TA_phenotypeX_pucks)
  
  # Test enrichment of phenotype_X in the TLS
  TLS_results <- enrichment_permutation_test(P107TA_phenotypeX_md, n_perm = n_perm, clonotype = phenotype)
  TLS_results$pval
  
  # Print plot only if the phenotype is significant
  if(TLS_results$pval <= 0.05){
    # Plot the fraction of beads with phenotype_X in TLS vs all other phenotypes in TLS for each puck.
    # In other words, plot Cc/Call and Ac/Aall
    plot <- P107TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
      group_by(tcr_label, area_label, orig.ident) %>%
      dplyr::count() %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = area_label, values_from = "n") %>%
      # Some values are supposed to be 0 but are NA due to dplyr::count
      replace(is.na(.), 0) %>%
      # Calculate Call and Aall
      mutate(all_areas = sum(area_of_interest, other_area),
      # Calculate Cc/Call and Ac/All
             fraction = area_of_interest/all_areas) %>%
      select(tcr_label, fraction, orig.ident) %>%
      ggplot() +
      geom_boxplot(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, fill = tcr_label)) +
      geom_point(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, color = orig.ident)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("Phenotype of interest: ", phenotype)) +
      ylab("Fraction in the TLS")
    
    print(plot)
  }
  
  # KEEP THIS ORDER, or the names of the list will be in the wrong order later
  TLS_phenotype_results.list <- append(TLS_phenotype_results.list, list(TLS_results))
}

title: 9  107T Tumor: Clone enrichment
text:
10.4 Permutation test logic:
Hypothesis: Our null hypothesis claims that TCR_X is not more enriched in the tumor than any other TCR The alternative hypothesis claims that TCR_Xs is more enriched in the tumor than any other TCR
Take into account the non-independent variable of puck identity by permuting sample label within the puck. e.g. say Puck30 has 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 has 20 TCR_X beads. After permutation, Puck30 will still have 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 20 TCR_X beads.
Test statistic:
Enrichment = (number of beads for TCR_X in the structure/total number of beads for TCR_X across all structures)/(number of beads for all other clonotypes in the structure/ total number of beads for all other clonotypes across all structures) - 1
In other words, enrichment = (fraction of TCR_X in the tumor) / (fraction of all other clones in the tumor) - 1

    filter(tcr_label == "TCR_of_interest") %>%
    distinct(orig.ident) %>%
    pull(orig.ident)

  P107TA_TCRX_md <- P107TA_TCRX_md %>%
    filter(orig.ident %in% P107TA_TCRX_pucks)
  
  # Test enrichment of TCR_X in the tumor
  tumor_results <- enrichment_permutation_test(P107TA_TCRX_md, n_perm = n_perm, clonotype = clone)
  tumor_results$pval

title: 10  107T Tumor: Phenotype enrichment
text:
11.4 Permutation test logic:
Hypothesis: Our null hypothesis claims that TCR_X is not more enriched in the tumor than any other TCR The alternative hypothesis claims that TCR_Xs is more enriched in the tumor than any other TCR
Take into account the non-independent variable of puck identity by permuting sample label within the puck. e.g. say Puck30 has 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 has 20 TCR_X beads. After permutation, Puck30 will still have 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 20 TCR_X beads.
Test statistic: Enrichment = (number of beads for TCR_X in the structure/total number of beads for TCR_X across all structures)/(number of beads for all other clonotypes in the structure/ total number of beads for all other clonotypes across all structures) - 1
In other words, enrichment = (fraction of TCR_X in the tumor) / (fraction of all other clones in the tumor) - 1
Note: this logic applies to specific clonotypes, phenotypes of clones, and specificity of clones

11.5 Get phenotypes seen at a count of 5 or more across all pucks

# Remove metadata columns that other pucks don't have
P107TA_15_RNA@meta.data <- P107TA_15_RNA@meta.data %>%
  select(-largest_clone_id, -puck15_count, -log_puck15_count)
P107TA_18_RNA@meta.data <- P107TA_18_RNA@meta.data %>%
  select(-largest_clone_id, -puck18_count, -log_puck18_count)
P107TA_20_RNA@meta.data <- P107TA_20_RNA@meta.data %>%
  select(-largest_clone_id, -puck20_count, -log_puck20_count)

P107TA_md <- do.call(rbind, list(P107TA_15_RNA@meta.data, P107TA_18_RNA@meta.data, P107TA_20_RNA@meta.data))

# These two functions work for grabbing phenotypes too- with some changes.
unique_phenotypes <- get_unique_productive_clones(P107TA_md$PrimaryCluster)

# Rename PrimaryCluster to clone_id so count_clones_by_annotated_area() works
temp <- P107TA_md %>%
  select(PrimaryCluster, area) %>%
  rename("clone_id" = "PrimaryCluster")

P107TA_filtered_phenotypes <- count_clones_by_annotated_area(temp, unique_phenotypes) %>%
  group_by(clone_id) %>%
  summarize(sum_count = sum(count)) %>%
  filter(sum_count >= 5) %>%
  pull(clone_id)

title: 10  107T Tumor: Phenotype enrichment
text:
11.7 Permute

# Save results in a list
tumor_phenotype_results.list <- list()

for(phenotype in P107TA_filtered_phenotypes){
  P107TA_phenotypeX_md <- P107TA_md %>%
    # Select columns of interest
    select(orig.ident, PrimaryCluster, barcode, area) %>%
    # Label phenotype_X containing beads as "interesting" and label all other beads as "other".
    # note: spatial-unique clonotypes will be labelled as "other".
    mutate(tcr_label = case_when(str_detect(PrimaryCluster, paste0(phenotype, "($|,)")) ~ "TCR_of_interest",
                                 T ~ "other_tcr"),
           # Label the tumor as "interesting" and other areas as "other"
           area_label = case_when(area == given_area ~ "area_of_interest",
                                  T ~ "other_area"))
  
  # Find pucks in which phenotype_X is observed
  P107TA_phenotypeX_pucks <- P107TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(tcr_label == "TCR_of_interest") %>%
    distinct(orig.ident) %>%
    pull(orig.ident)
  
  # Filter for pucks where phenotype_X is observed
  P107TA_phenotypeX_md <- P107TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(orig.ident %in% P107TA_phenotypeX_pucks)
  
  # Test enrichment of phenotype_X in the tumor
  tumor_results <- enrichment_permutation_test(P107TA_phenotypeX_md, n_perm = n_perm, clonotype = phenotype)
  tumor_results$pval
  
  # Print plot only if the phenotype is significant
  if(tumor_results$pval <= 0.05){
    # Plot the fraction of beads with phenotype_X in TLS vs all other phenotypes in TLS for each puck.
    # In other words, plot Cc/Call and Ac/Aall
    plot <- P107TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
      group_by(tcr_label, area_label, orig.ident) %>%
      dplyr::count() %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = area_label, values_from = "n") %>%
      # Some values are supposed to be 0 but are NA due to dplyr::count
      replace(is.na(.), 0) %>%
      # Calculate Call and Aall
      mutate(all_areas = sum(area_of_interest, other_area),
      # Calculate Cc/Call and Ac/All
             fraction = area_of_interest/all_areas) %>%
      select(tcr_label, fraction, orig.ident) %>%
      ggplot() +
      geom_boxplot(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, fill = tcr_label)) +
      geom_point(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, color = orig.ident)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("Phenotype of interest: ", phenotype)) +
      ylab("Fraction in the tumor")
    
    print(plot)
  }
  
  # KEEP THIS ORDER, or the names of the list will be in the wrong order later
  tumor_phenotype_results.list <- append(tumor_phenotype_results.list, list(tumor_results))
}

title: 12  108TA Boundary: Phenotype enrichment
text:
13.4 Permutation test logic:
Hypothesis: Our null hypothesis claims that TCR_X is not more enriched in the boundary than any other TCR The alternative hypothesis claims that TCR_Xs is more enriched in the boundary than any other TCR
Take into account the non-independent variable of puck identity by permuting sample label within the puck. e.g. say Puck30 has 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 has 20 TCR_X beads. After permutation, Puck30 will still have 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 20 TCR_X beads.
Test statistic: Enrichment = (number of beads for TCR_X in the structure/total number of beads for TCR_X across all structures)/(number of beads for all other clonotypes in the structure/ total number of beads for all other clonotypes across all structures) - 1
In other words, enrichment = (fraction of TCR_X in the boundary) / (fraction of all other clones in the boundary) - 1
Note: this logic applies to specific clonotypes, phenotypes of clones, and specificity of clones

13.5 Get phenotypes seen at a count of 5 or more across all pucks

# Merge data from all pucks together
P108TA_md <- do.call(rbind, list(P108TA_26_RNA@meta.data, P108TA_29_RNA@meta.data, P108TA_30_RNA@meta.data, P108TA_32_RNA@meta.data))

# These two functions work for grabbing phenotypes too- with some changes.
unique_phenotypes <- get_unique_productive_clones(P108TA_md$PrimaryCluster)

# Rename PrimaryCluster to clone_id so count_clones_by_annotated_area() works
temp <- P108TA_md %>%
  select(PrimaryCluster, area) %>%
  rename("clone_id" = "PrimaryCluster")

P108TA_filtered_phenotypes <- count_clones_by_annotated_area(temp, unique_phenotypes) %>%
  group_by(clone_id) %>%
  summarize(sum_count = sum(count)) %>%
  filter(sum_count >= 5) %>%
  pull(clone_id)

title: 12  108TA Boundary: Phenotype enrichment
text:
13.7 Permute

# Save results in a list
boundary_phenotype_results.list <- list()

for(phenotype in P108TA_filtered_phenotypes){
  P108TA_phenotypeX_md <- P108TA_md %>%
    # Select columns of interest
    select(orig.ident, PrimaryCluster, barcode, area) %>%
    # Label phenotype_X containing beads as "interesting" and label all other beads as "other".
    # note: spatial-unique clonotypes will be labelled as "other".
    mutate(tcr_label = case_when(str_detect(PrimaryCluster, paste0(phenotype, "($|,)")) ~ "TCR_of_interest",
                                 T ~ "other_tcr"),
           # Label the boundary as "interesting" and other areas as "other"
           area_label = case_when(area == given_area ~ "area_of_interest",
                                  T ~ "other_area"))
  
  # Find pucks in which phenotype_X is observed
  P108TA_phenotypeX_pucks <- P108TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(tcr_label == "TCR_of_interest") %>%
    distinct(orig.ident) %>%
    pull(orig.ident)
  
  # Filter for pucks where phenotype_X is observed
  P108TA_phenotypeX_md <- P108TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(orig.ident %in% P108TA_phenotypeX_pucks)

  # Test enrichment of phenotype_X in the boundary
  boundary_results <- enrichment_permutation_test(P108TA_phenotypeX_md, n_perm = n_perm, clonotype = phenotype)
  boundary_results$pval
  
  # Print plot only if the phenotype is significant
  if(boundary_results$pval <= 0.05){
    # Plot the fraction of beads with phenotype_X in TLS vs all other phenotypes in TLS for each puck.
    # In other words, plot Cc/Call and Ac/Aall
    plot <- P108TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
      group_by(tcr_label, area_label, orig.ident) %>%
      dplyr::count() %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = area_label, values_from = "n") %>%
      # Some values are supposed to be 0 but are NA due to dplyr::count
      replace(is.na(.), 0) %>%
      # Calculate Call and Aall
      mutate(all_areas = sum(area_of_interest, other_area),
      # Calculate Cc/Call and Ac/All
             fraction = area_of_interest/all_areas) %>%
      select(tcr_label, fraction, orig.ident) %>%
      ggplot() +
      geom_boxplot(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, fill = tcr_label)) +
      geom_point(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, color = orig.ident)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("Phenotype of interest: ", phenotype)) +
      ylab("Fraction in the boundary")
    
    print(plot)
  }
  
  # KEEP THIS ORDER, or the names of the list will be in the wrong order later
  boundary_phenotype_results.list <- append(boundary_phenotype_results.list, list(boundary_results))
}

title: 13  108TA TLS: Clone enrichment
text:
14.4 Permutation test logic:
Hypothesis: Our null hypothesis claims that TCR_X is not more enriched in the TLS than any other TCR The alternative hypothesis claims that TCR_Xs is more enriched in the TLS than any other TCR
Take into account the non-independent variable of puck identity by permuting sample label within the puck. e.g. say Puck30 has 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 has 20 TCR_X beads. After permutation, Puck30 will still have 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 20 TCR_X beads.
Test statistic:
Enrichment = (number of beads for TCR_X in the structure/total number of beads for TCR_X across all structures)/(number of beads for all other clonotypes in the structure/ total number of beads for all other clonotypes across all structures) - 1
In other words, enrichment = (fraction of TCR_X in the TLS) / (fraction of all other clones in the TLS) - 1

# Merge data from all pucks together
P108TA_md <- do.call(rbind, list(P108TA_26_RNA@meta.data, P108TA_29_RNA@meta.data, P108TA_30_RNA@meta.data, P108TA_32_RNA@meta.data))

unique_clones <- get_unique_productive_clones(P108TA_md$clone_id)

P108TA_filtered_clones <- count_clones_by_annotated_area(P108TA_md, unique_clones) %>%
  group_by(clone_id) %>%
  summarize(sum_count = sum(count)) %>%
  filter(sum_count >= 5) %>%

14.6 Rename individual TLS and filter for beads containing TCRs

# Rename individual TLS to "TLS"
P108TA_md <- P108TA_md %>%
  mutate(area = case_when(area %in% c("TLS1", "TLS2", "TLS3") ~ "TLS",
                                  T ~ area)) %>%
  # Filter for beads with TCRs
  filter(!is.na(clone_id))

title: 13  108TA TLS: Clone enrichment
text:
14.7 Run the permutation test

# Save results in a list
tls_clone_results.list <- list()

for(clone in P108TA_filtered_clones){
  P108TA_TCRX_md <- P108TA_md %>%
    # Select columns of interest
    select(orig.ident, clone_id, barcode, area) %>%
    # Label TCR_X containing beads as "interesting" and label all other beads as "other".
    mutate(tcr_label = case_when(str_detect(clone_id, paste0(clone, "($|,)")) ~ "TCR_of_interest",
                                 T ~ "other_tcr"),
           # Label the TLS as "interesting" and other areas as "other"
           area_label = case_when(area == given_area ~ "area_of_interest",
                                  T ~ "other_area"))
  
  # Find pucks in which TCR_X is not observed
  P108TA_TCRX_pucks <- P108TA_TCRX_md %>%
    filter(tcr_label == "TCR_of_interest") %>%
    distinct(orig.ident) %>%
    pull(orig.ident)
  
  # Remove pucks where TCR_X is not observed
  P108TA_TCRX_md <- P108TA_TCRX_md %>%
    filter(orig.ident %in% P108TA_TCRX_pucks)
  
  # Test enrichment of TCR_X in the TLS
  tls_results <- enrichment_permutation_test(P108TA_TCRX_md, n_perm = n_perm, clonotype = clone)
  tls_results$pval
  
  # Print plot only if the clone is significant
  if(tls_results$pval <= 0.05){
    # Plot the fraction of TCR_X in TLS vs all other TCRs in TLS for each puck.
    # In other words, plot Cc/Call and Ac/Aall
    plot <- P108TA_TCRX_md %>%
      group_by(tcr_label, area_label, orig.ident) %>%
      dplyr::count() %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = area_label, values_from = "n") %>%
      # Some values are supposed to be 0 but are NA due to dplyr::count
      replace(is.na(.), 0) %>%
      # Calculate Call and Aall
      mutate(all_areas = sum(area_of_interest, other_area),
      # Calculate Cc/Call and Ac/All
             fraction = area_of_interest/all_areas) %>%
      select(tcr_label, fraction, orig.ident) %>%
      ggplot() +
      geom_boxplot(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, fill = tcr_label)) +
      geom_point(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, color = orig.ident)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("TCR of interest: ", clone)) +
      ylab("Fraction in the TLS")
    
    print(plot)
  }
  
  # KEEP THIS ORDER, or the names of the list will be in the wrong order later
  tls_clone_results.list <- append(tls_clone_results.list, list(tls_results))
}

# Save results in a list
TLS_phenotype_results.list <- list()

for(phenotype in P108TA_filtered_phenotypes){
  P108TA_phenotypeX_md <- P108TA_md %>%
    # Select columns of interest
    select(orig.ident, PrimaryCluster, barcode, area) %>%
    # Label phenotype_X containing beads as "interesting" and label all other beads as "other".
    # note: spatial-unique clonotypes will be labelled as "other" & str_detect IS case sensitive!
    mutate(tcr_label = case_when(str_detect(PrimaryCluster, paste0(phenotype, "($|,)")) ~ "TCR_of_interest",
                                 T ~ "other_tcr"),
           # Label the TLS as "interesting" and other areas as "other"
           area_label = case_when(area == given_area ~ "area_of_interest",
                                  T ~ "other_area"))
  
  # Find pucks in which phenotype_X is observed
  P108TA_phenotypeX_pucks <- P108TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(tcr_label == "TCR_of_interest") %>%
    distinct(orig.ident) %>%
    pull(orig.ident)
  
  # Filter for pucks where phenotype_X is observed
  P108TA_phenotypeX_md <- P108TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(orig.ident %in% P108TA_phenotypeX_pucks)
  
  # Test enrichment of phenotype_X in the TLS
  TLS_results <- enrichment_permutation_test(P108TA_phenotypeX_md, n_perm = n_perm, clonotype = phenotype)
  
  # Print plot only if the phenotype is significant
  if(TLS_results$pval <= 0.05){
    # Plot the fraction of beads with phenotype_X in TLS vs all other phenotypes in TLS for each puck.
    # In other words, plot Cc/Call and Ac/Aall
    plot <- P108TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
      group_by(tcr_label, area_label, orig.ident) %>%
      dplyr::count() %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = area_label, values_from = "n") %>%
      # Some values are supposed to be 0 but are NA due to dplyr::count
      replace(is.na(.), 0) %>%
      # Calculate Call and Aall
      mutate(all_areas = sum(area_of_interest, other_area),
      # Calculate Cc/Call and Ac/All
             fraction = area_of_interest/all_areas) %>%
      select(tcr_label, fraction, orig.ident) %>%
      ggplot() +
      geom_boxplot(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, fill = tcr_label)) +
      geom_point(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, color = orig.ident)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("Phenotype of interest: ", phenotype)) +
      ylab("Fraction in the TLS")
    
    print(plot)
  }
  
  # KEEP THIS ORDER, or the names of the list will be in the wrong order later
  TLS_phenotype_results.list <- append(TLS_phenotype_results.list, list(TLS_results))
}

title: 15  108TA Tumor: Clone enrichment
text:
16.4 Permutation test logic:
Hypothesis: Our null hypothesis claims that TCR_X is not more enriched in the tumor than any other TCR The alternative hypothesis claims that TCR_Xs is more enriched in the tumor than any other TCR
Take into account the non-independent variable of puck identity by permuting sample label within the puck. e.g. say Puck30 has 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 has 20 TCR_X beads. After permutation, Puck30 will still have 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 20 TCR_X beads.
Test statistic:
Enrichment = (number of beads for TCR_X in the structure/total number of beads for TCR_X across all structures)/(number of beads for all other clonotypes in the structure/ total number of beads for all other clonotypes across all structures) - 1
In other words, enrichment = (fraction of TCR_X in the tumor) / (fraction of all other clones in the tumor) - 1

title: 15  108TA Tumor: Clone enrichment
text:
16.7 Permute

# Save results in a list
tumor_clone_results.list <- list()

for(clone in P108TA_filtered_clones){
  P108TA_TCRX_md <- P108TA_md %>%
    # Select columns of interest
    select(orig.ident, clone_id, barcode, area) %>%
    # Label TCR_X containing beads as "interesting" and label all other beads as "other".
    mutate(tcr_label = case_when(str_detect(clone_id, paste0(clone, "($|,)")) ~ "TCR_of_interest",
                                 T ~ "other_tcr"),
           # Label the tumor as "interesting" and other areas as "other"
           area_label = case_when(area == given_area ~ "area_of_interest",
                                  T ~ "other_area"))
  
  # Find pucks in which TCR_X is not observed
  P108TA_TCRX_pucks <- P108TA_TCRX_md %>%
    filter(tcr_label == "TCR_of_interest") %>%
    distinct(orig.ident) %>%
    pull(orig.ident)
  
  # Remove pucks where TCR_X is not observed
  P108TA_TCRX_md <- P108TA_TCRX_md %>%
    filter(orig.ident %in% P108TA_TCRX_pucks)
  
  # Test enrichment of TCR_X in the tumor
  tumor_results <- enrichment_permutation_test(P108TA_TCRX_md, n_perm = n_perm, clonotype = clone)
  tumor_results$pval

  # Print plot only if the clone is significant
  if(tumor_results$pval <= 0.05){
    # Plot the fraction of TCR_X in TLS vs all other TCRs in TLS for each puck.
    # In other words, plot Cc/Call and Ac/Aall
    plot <- P108TA_TCRX_md %>%
      group_by(tcr_label, area_label, orig.ident) %>%
      dplyr::count() %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = area_label, values_from = "n") %>%
      # Some values are supposed to be 0 but are NA due to dplyr::count
      replace(is.na(.), 0) %>%
      # Calculate Call and Aall
      mutate(all_areas = sum(area_of_interest, other_area),
      # Calculate Cc/Call and Ac/All
             fraction = area_of_interest/all_areas) %>%
      select(tcr_label, fraction, orig.ident) %>%
      ggplot() +
      geom_boxplot(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, fill = tcr_label)) +
      geom_point(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, color = orig.ident)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("TCR of interest: ", clone)) +
      ylab("Fraction in the tumor")
    
    print(plot)
  }
  
  # KEEP THIS ORDER, or the names of the list will be in the wrong order later
  tumor_clone_results.list <- append(tumor_clone_results.list, list(tumor_results))
}

title: 16  108TA Tumor: Phenotype enrichment
text:
17.4 Permutation test logic:
Hypothesis: Our null hypothesis claims that TCR_X is not more enriched in the tumor than any other TCR The alternative hypothesis claims that TCR_Xs is more enriched in the tumor than any other TCR
Take into account the non-independent variable of puck identity by permuting sample label within the puck. e.g. say Puck30 has 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 has 20 TCR_X beads. After permutation, Puck30 will still have 10 TCR_X beads, and Puck32 20 TCR_X beads.
Test statistic: Enrichment = (number of beads for TCR_X in the structure/total number of beads for TCR_X across all structures)/(number of beads for all other clonotypes in the structure/ total number of beads for all other clonotypes across all structures) - 1
In other words, enrichment = (fraction of TCR_X in the tumor) / (fraction of all other clones in the tumor) - 1
Note: this logic applies to specific clonotypes, phenotypes of clones, and specificity of clones

17.5 Get phenotypes seen at a count of 5 or more across all pucks

# Merge data from all pucks together
P108TA_md <- do.call(rbind, list(P108TA_26_RNA@meta.data, P108TA_29_RNA@meta.data, P108TA_30_RNA@meta.data, P108TA_32_RNA@meta.data))

# These two functions work for grabbing phenotypes too- with some changes.
unique_phenotypes <- get_unique_productive_clones(P108TA_md$PrimaryCluster)

# Rename PrimaryCluster to clone_id so count_clones_by_annotated_area() works
temp <- P108TA_md %>%
  select(PrimaryCluster, area) %>%
  rename("clone_id" = "PrimaryCluster")

P108TA_filtered_phenotypes <- count_clones_by_annotated_area(temp, unique_phenotypes) %>%
  group_by(clone_id) %>%
  summarize(sum_count = sum(count)) %>%
  filter(sum_count >= 5) %>%
  pull(clone_id)

title: 16  108TA Tumor: Phenotype enrichment
text:
17.7 Permute

# Save results in a list
tumor_phenotype_results.list <- list()

for(phenotype in P108TA_filtered_phenotypes){
  P108TA_phenotypeX_md <- P108TA_md %>%
    # Select columns of interest
    select(orig.ident, PrimaryCluster, barcode, area) %>%
    # Label phenotype_X containing beads as "interesting" and label all other beads as "other".
    # note: spatial-unique clonotypes will be labelled as "other".
    mutate(tcr_label = case_when(str_detect(PrimaryCluster, paste0(phenotype, "($|,)")) ~ "TCR_of_interest",
                                 T ~ "other_tcr"),
           # Label the tumor as "interesting" and other areas as "other"
           area_label = case_when(area == given_area ~ "area_of_interest",
                                  T ~ "other_area"))
  
  # Find pucks in which phenotype_X is observed
  P108TA_phenotypeX_pucks <- P108TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(tcr_label == "TCR_of_interest") %>%
    distinct(orig.ident) %>%
    pull(orig.ident)
  
  # Filter for pucks where phenotype_X is observed
  P108TA_phenotypeX_md <- P108TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
    filter(orig.ident %in% P108TA_phenotypeX_pucks)
  
  # Test enrichment of phenotype_X in the tumor
  tumor_results <- enrichment_permutation_test(P108TA_phenotypeX_md, n_perm = n_perm, clonotype = phenotype)
  tumor_results$pval
  
  # Print plot only if the phenotype is significant
  if(tumor_results$pval <= 0.05){
    # Plot the fraction of beads with phenotype_X in TLS vs all other phenotypes in TLS for each puck.
    # In other words, plot Cc/Call and Ac/Aall
    plot <- P108TA_phenotypeX_md %>%
      group_by(tcr_label, area_label, orig.ident) %>%
      dplyr::count() %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = area_label, values_from = "n") %>%
      # Some values are supposed to be 0 but are NA due to dplyr::count
      replace(is.na(.), 0) %>%
      # Calculate Call and Aall
      mutate(all_areas = sum(area_of_interest, other_area),
      # Calculate Cc/Call and Ac/All
             fraction = area_of_interest/all_areas) %>%
      select(tcr_label, fraction, orig.ident) %>%
      ggplot() +
      geom_boxplot(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, fill = tcr_label)) +
      geom_point(aes(x = tcr_label, y = fraction, color = orig.ident)) +
      ggtitle(paste0("Phenotype of interest: ", phenotype)) +
      ylab("Fraction in the tumor")
    
    print(plot)
  }
  
  # KEEP THIS ORDER, or the names of the list will be in the wrong order later
  tumor_phenotype_results.list <- append(tumor_phenotype_results.list, list(tumor_results))
}
